title: Fluffy Indian Fish Curry
description: A savory one‑pot curry that keeps fish light and fluffy, accented with toasted coconut flakes and a gentle steam finish.

ingredients.name: Firm white fish fillets (cod or tilapia)

instructions: Pat the fish fillets dry and season with salt. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat and add cumin seeds; let them splutter.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and grated ginger; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato puree and cook until the mixture thickens and oil separates, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the ground coriander powder and garam masala; cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in the coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer, then add the seasoned fish fillets.
Cover the pot and let the fish steam in the simmering liquid for 8–10 minutes until opaque.
While the fish cooks, toast the coconut flakes in a dry skillet over medium heat until lightly golden; set aside.
Once the fish is done, adjust seasoning with salt and a splash of lemon juice, then gently fold in the toasted coconut flakes for a fluffy texture.
Gently lift the fish with a slotted spoon and let it rest for a minute before serving.
